title: rosa × andegavensis var. kosinsciana
intro: Rosa × andegavensis var. kosinsciana, also known as the Kosin's Alpine Rose, is a rare and endangered subspecies of rose that is native to the mountainous regions of the Czech Republic. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa × andegavensis var. kosinsciana include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
